concerns.text: FR-001 references 'See US-1', but the User Story 1 title is 'Data Ingestion and LLM Adoption Classification'. The requirement text matches the story, but the cross-reference ID 'US-1' is not explicitly defined as a unique identifier in the text (e.g., 'User Story 1' vs 'US-1'). While contextually clear, strict internal consistency requires the ID format to be unique and resolvable. The text uses 'User Story 1' in headers but 'US-1' in requirements. This is a terminology inconsistency.

FR-002 references 'See US-1'. Similar to FR-001, the cross-reference uses 'US-1' while the section header uses 'User Story 1'. Ensure consistent identifier usage (e.g., always 'User Story 1' or always 'US-1') throughout the document to avoid ambiguity in traceability.

FR-003 references 'See US-2'. The User Story 2 header is 'User Story 2 - Statistical Analysis...'. The requirement uses 'US-2'. This is a terminology inconsistency between the section header and the cross-reference ID.
FR-004 references 'See US-2'. Same terminology inconsistency as above: 'US-2' vs 'User Story 2'.
FR-005 references 'See US-2'. Same terminology inconsistency as above: 'US-2' vs 'User Story 2'.
FR-006 references 'See US-3'. The User Story 3 header is 'User Story 3 - Visualization...'. The requirement uses 'US-3'. Same terminology inconsistency.
SC-001 references 'See US-1'. Same terminology inconsistency: 'US-1' vs 'User Story 1'.
